spoken_briefing:
This week in AI tools for specialty contractors and small general contractors.

Starting with bidding and estimating. STACK Construction Technologies is a cloud-based estimating platform designed from the ground up for trade subcontractors, letting crews move from uploaded plans to bid-ready numbers faster than traditional tools. Pricing starts at two thousand nine hundred ninety-nine dollars per year, and it connects with Procore for budget and cost code synchronization.

Togal dot AI has updated its pricing for 2026, now offering a Growth plan at two hundred ninety-nine dollars per user per month billed annually, with its Business tier on custom quote for teams of four or more. Its AI-driven floor plan takeoffs claim ninety-eight percent accuracy, with estimators completing full architectural takeoffs in twelve minutes instead of hours. The platform features a three D visualization tool, AI-powered image search, and TogalCHAT for querying project documents. Coastal Construction documented saving fourteen and a half hours per plan set, adding up to roughly one million dollars in annual cost savings. NC Painting used it to scale to sixty bids per month in under sixty days.

BidIntell is a pre-bid decision platform built specifically for specialty subcontractors who need to filter which invitations are worth pursuing. It scores incoming bid requests on a one-hundred-point BidIndex based on location fit, contract terms, your relationship history with the GC, and trade match. Pricing starts at forty-nine dollars per user per month, making it one of the most accessible AI tools on the market right now.

Beam AI from iBeam combines automated quantity takeoffs with human review to ensure accuracy before the numbers go out the door, covering HVAC, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, and civil trades. Pricing is custom-quoted based on volume.

Downtobid is an AI-powered bid management platform that reads construction plans, identifies every trade scope automatically, and sends personalized bid invitations to verified local subcontractors from a database of more than two hundred fifty thousand commercial subs. General contractors start at one hundred forty-nine dollars per month, and subcontractors accessing the full platform pay two hundred ninety-nine dollars per month. Personalized invites using the platform generate two times more responses than generic portal emails.

Moving to submittal and RFI tracking. Trunk Tools is an AI-native construction platform built around the problem of unstructured project data. Its TrunkSubmittal product reviews submittals against specifications in seconds and flags discrepancies before they become costly. Cleveland Construction reported saving more than seven hundred ninety hours and sixty thousand dollars across four projects, and pilot firm Torcon saved more than four hundred fifty hours over four months using Trunk Tools agents for RFI management and submittal review. In May 2026, Trunk Tools launched a new integration with Autodesk Forma, and Gilbane Building Company has begun rolling out Trunk Tools AI agents across all its jobsites. Pricing is by custom quote.

Procore's AI platform, known as Procore Helix, now includes an Agent Builder that lets teams automate RFI drafting, submittal routing, and daily log generation without any coding required. The Procore Assist feature answers questions from specs, submittals, and building codes in seconds, and the platform supports multilingual field teams. Procore is priced on annual construction volume and integrates broadly with Autodesk, Sage, and most major accounting and field tools.

Civils dot AI extracts data directly from drawings, specs, and building codes to automate RFI generation and compliance tracking. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams, and pricing is by custom quote.

Now for field management and daily reporting. CompanyCam is a photo-first field documentation platform that has added meaningful AI capabilities including its Walkthrough Note feature, where a superintendent walks the site, speaks observations, and snaps photos, and AI automatically assembles a structured daily report. Plans start at ninety-nine dollars per month with a three-user minimum, and the platform integrates with Procore, JobNimbus, and a growing list of CRM and estimating tools. It is built for specialty contractors and trades who document heavy.

eSUB is the subcontractor-exclusive field management platform that connects the field to the office for commercial specialty subs. It handles daily reports, change orders, RFIs, time cards, submittals, and purchase orders with integrations to Sage 300 and major accounting platforms. eSUB is positioned as a more affordable alternative to Procore for subs who do not need GC-side features. Pricing is by custom quote.

Buildots uses helmet-mounted cameras and computer vision to capture site progress data and compare it against the project schedule in real time, flagging delays and installation errors before they compound. It is built for GC teams on complex commercial projects and pricing is custom-quoted by project size.

Raken is a field documentation and reporting platform purpose-built for field crews, covering daily reports, time tracking, toolbox talks, and production tracking. Field workers log manhour data with daily quantities installed, reducing cost code entry time compared to paper logs. Pricing starts at fifteen dollars per user per month for the Lite plan and twenty-five dollars per user per month for Pro, making it one of the most affordable field reporting entry points available.

BuildOps launched its Projects module in May 2026, giving specialty contractors an AI-native system to run multi-month construction work from kickoff to closeout alongside their service operations. The platform's embedded AI, called OpsAI, handles scheduling, document management, field coordination, and daily reporting in one unified system. BuildOps raised one hundred twenty-seven million dollars in a Series C round in 2026, reaching a one billion dollar valuation. Pricing is by custom quote and it is built exclusively for specialty trade contractors.

InspectMind AI is a Y Combinator-backed platform that generates field reports, punch lists, safety inspections, and site walkthroughs in minutes using AI. Its Field Reports product captures photos and voice notes on mobile, works offline, and produces structured reports at one hundred dollars per user per month. Plan checking starts at one hundred dollars per review. It integrates with Procore and Autodesk Construction Cloud.

For time and materials and billing automation. HCSS HeavyJob is the go-to standard for heavy and civil specialty contractors doing time-and-material work. It pulls T and M billing data directly from field time cards and generates accurate invoices with minimal manual effort. HCSS recently expanded HeavyJob for utility contractors to manage work orders end to end. Pricing ranges from sixty to over four thousand dollars per user per year depending on modules selected. It integrates with major accounting systems.

FOUNDATION Software's T and M Billing Module captures every billable expense, automates markups, and produces client-ready invoices for time-and-material contracts. It is designed specifically for specialty subcontractors and integrates deeply with FOUNDATION's construction accounting suite. Pricing is custom-quoted.

SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool that handles labor verification and safety compliance simultaneously. Pricing is custom-quoted based on site count and worker volume.

Rhumbix is a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work and out-of-scope activities in real time. It integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks Online, and ADP, and is built for trade contractors and self-performing divisions who need to separate T and M work from fixed-price contracts. Pricing is custom-quoted based on user count.

Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking, and uses AI to validate waivers and flag errors before they hold up payment. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average, saving the equivalent of seven days per week across their billing workflow. Siteline integrates with Viewpoint Vista, Procore Pay, Textura, and GCPay. Pricing is custom-quoted based on billing volume.

On change order and document management. Document Crunch is the AI contract review platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced its acquisition of Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 and full integration planned into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem. Pricing is by custom quote and it serves both GC and specialty sub teams.

Procore's change order management with AI Assist lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time, all inside the same platform where the schedule and budget already live. Pricing is on annual construction volume.

Pelles AI is a newer generative AI platform that can create RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is custom-quoted.

Now for proposal generation and pre-bid analysis. BidIntell's pre-bid scoring also anchors the proposal phase, helping specialty subs invest estimating hours only on the invitations that fit their win profile before a single takeoff is started.

Flowcase is an AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates. It is especially strong for subs competing for design-assist and qualifications-based work. Pricing is custom-quoted.

ContraVault takes a team from opportunity qualification all the way to a submission-ready proposal using AI to assemble, draft, and review content with minimal manual effort. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams competing for complex project awards. Pricing is by custom quote. A 2025 Dodge Construction Network survey found that average bid preparation time has dropped from thirty-four hours to fourteen hours per project for contractors using AI-powered estimating, and those contractors reported nineteen percent fewer cost overruns and twenty-two percent fewer change orders traced to estimating errors.

On project scheduling with AI. ALICE Technologies is the most thoroughly documented AI scheduling platform in commercial construction. It simulates millions of possible build sequences to surface the most time and cost efficient path, and adjusts dynamically when conditions on the job change. In April 2026, ALICE formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company to bring generative scheduling to capital project clients across infrastructure, data centers, and energy, introducing the technology to more than thirty-five clients and achieving schedule accelerations of up to twenty percent. A McKinsey data center client reduced its entire construction schedule by forty percent. Earlier documented results include a seventeen percent reduction in project duration, fourteen percent labor cost savings, and twelve percent equipment cost savings. Pricing is by custom quote, and ALICE works alongside Primavera P6 and Oracle Primavera Cloud schedules.

Phoenix Project Manager is built for specialty contractors who need resource-loaded critical path schedules without the complexity of Primavera P6. Its labor histogram view lets electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors see workforce demand across their entire project portfolio. Starting at eighteen hundred dollars per year, it is one of the most accessible CPM scheduling tools purpose-built for specialty subs.

Oracle Primavera P6 remains the industry standard on large complex projects at roughly twenty-one hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars per user per year, and its AI capabilities are being extended through ALICE and third-party integrations.

nPlan is an AI construction schedule forecasting platform trained on seven hundred fifty thousand historical construction schedules representing over two trillion dollars of construction spend. It forecasts the uncertainty of every activity in a schedule, automatically identifies schedule integrity issues, and creates new schedules from scope documentation using generative AI, giving project controls teams a level of accuracy unattainable by manual analysis. Pricing is by custom quote.

SmartPM is an AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to flag delays, variances, and critical path risks in real time. It transforms raw scheduling data into automated reports and actionable dashboards without requiring a scheduling specialist. Pricing starts at four hundred dollars per project per month, and it integrates with Autodesk Construction Cloud and Procore.

Outbuild is an AI-enabled collaborative scheduling platform trusted by four hundred or more customers with over fifty billion dollars in active projects. Teams create master schedules and lookahead plans in a connected cloud environment where field updates sync back automatically while schedulers maintain control. Pricing scales based on annual construction volume rather than per user or per project, making it a practical option for growing specialty sub teams, with a thirty-day free trial available. It integrates with Procore.

Finally, safety and compliance monitoring. Intenseye is an AI video analytics platform that monitors existing camera feeds and detects more than fifty categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time, including PPE violations, restricted zone entry, and ergonomic hazards. The company has now raised over one hundred fifty-four million dollars in total funding and operates across more than twenty-five countries. It has released a new mobile app and is adding large language model capabilities. Pricing is by custom quote based on camera count.

Voxel AI offers AI-powered safety monitoring with total funding now at eighty million dollars including a forty-four million dollar Series B, and has expanded operations to seven new countries. Pricing is structured around camera count at an estimated thirty to fifty percent lower cost than comparable enterprise platforms.

Oracle launched its Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety in March 2026, adding predictive analytics that forecast where safety incidents are likely before they happen. It is aimed at large GC and owner teams running complex projects with exposure to high-frequency incident patterns.

Pillar Technologies uses connected environmental sensors alongside visual monitoring to deliver real-time construction site risk management, with alerts covering temperature, moisture, atmospheric conditions, and visual hazard detection. Pricing is custom-quoted and it is primarily built for general contractors.

DroneDeploy Safety AI analyzes drone imagery and site camera data against OSHA standards to detect construction hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations, with reports accurate to ninety-five percent. Beta customers reduced the occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to eighty-nine percent within three weeks of deployment. Pricing for the individual plan starts at three hundred forty-nine dollars per month billed annually, with advanced AI analytics on the six-hundred-forty-nine-dollar plan.

viAct transforms existing site cameras into AI safety monitors using more than two hundred specialized detection modules covering PPE violations, fall risks, unsafe acts at height, restricted zone breaches, and worker-equipment proximity hazards. It also functions as a digital EHS assistant that automates incident reports, generates compliance documentation, and delivers real-time safety trend analysis. Pricing starts at two hundred dollars per camera per module per month, with a fourteen-day free trial.

Fyld is an AI field operations platform that analyzes short video clips from jobsites to identify safety, quality, and delivery risks before they escalate. It raised forty-one million dollars in a Series B round in February 2026 and reported eighty-two percent year-over-year growth in 2025. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, and Emery Sapp and Sons. Contractors using the platform report reductions in serious workplace incidents of up to forty-eight percent. Pricing is by custom quote and it is built for specialty contractors and infrastructure operators managing field operations at scale.

Now for market context. On what owners and large GCs are expecting: major general contractors are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above ten million dollars. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has now doubled year over year, with thirty-eight percent of contractors reporting measurable business impact from AI, up from seventeen percent just one year ago, and ninety-four percent of those using AI plan to increase their usage in 2026.

On realistic adoption cost for a specialty sub with five to twenty project managers: a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs between fifteen thousand and eighty thousand dollars per year in software costs. The range depends heavily on whether the team chooses a bundled platform like Procore or assembles best-of-breed point solutions. Training represents an additional fifteen to twenty percent of the total investment and is the factor most consistently underestimated. Implementation typically takes sixty to ninety days before teams reach operational efficiency.

On documented ROI: the numbers from early adopters remain compelling. A 2025 Bluebeam industry report found that sixty-eight percent of AI early adopters in construction have saved at least fifty thousand dollars, and forty-six percent have recovered between five hundred and one thousand hours of labor. The average return across the industry is three and a half times the investment, with top performers reporting ten times ROI. Bid preparation time is down by as much as sixty percent. Clark Construction documented three point two million dollars in project savings through AI-assisted risk management on a single program. Firms using AI scheduling report twenty to thirty percent fewer project delays. ENR reports that contractors automating billing workflows recover eight to twelve administrative hours per week. The message for specialty subs is straightforward: the tools are commercially available today, the ROI is documented and growing, and the window to adopt before digital capability becomes a competitive prerequisite is closing fast.

tools.pricing: Included in Procore platform; priced on Annual Construction Volume (ACV)
tools.results: Serves 17,500+ customers; 45-90 minute reviews reduced to 10-15 minutes; full ROI typically within 60-90 days of adoption.
tools.integrations: Autodesk, Sage, Viewpoint, Trimble, 400+ third-party apps

Procore is priced on annual construction volume and integrates broadly with Autodesk, Sage, and most major accounting and field tools.

tools.pricing: $60-$4,000+/user/year depending on modules; custom enterprise pricing
tools.results: Announced at DISTRIBUTECH 2025 as the only end-to-end solution for utility contractor T&M work; contractors report significant payroll processing time reduction.
tools.integrations: Major construction accounting systems

tools.integrations: Native to FOUNDATION construction accounting; integrates with project management workflows

tools.name: SmartBarrel

tools.pricing: Custom quote based on site count, worker count, and hardware deployment
tools.results: Eliminates buddy-punching; provides accurate T&M labor documentation; adds PPE compliance monitoring layer to time tracking hardware.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with payroll and project management systems
tools.spoken: SmartBarrel combines AI biometric facial recognition for time tracking with PPE compliance monitoring at clock-in, giving specialty subs a dual-purpose tool that handles labor verification and safety compliance simultaneously. tools.name: Rhumbix
tools.description: Mobile-first workforce tracking and T&M billing platform for construction that automates T&M ticket generation from field time card data, captures unplanned work and out-of-scope activities in real time, and produces streamlined change order documentation.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on user count
tools.results: 50% reduction in T&M processing time; digital T&M tag processing reduced from 2-6 weeks to 1-2 days; cost overruns caught 2-3 weeks earlier than paper-based systems.
tools.integrations: Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks Online Advanced, ADP Workforce Now
tools.spoken: Rhumbix is a mobile-first time and materials tracking platform that automates T and M ticket generation directly from field time cards and captures unplanned work and out-of-scope activities in real time. It integrates with Procore, Autodesk Construction Cloud, QuickBooks Online, and ADP, and is built for trade contractors and self-performing divisions who need to separate T and M work from fixed-price contracts. Pricing is custom-quoted based on user count.

tools.name: Siteline
tools.description: Construction billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. Automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking. Uses AI to validate waivers and flag missing signatures, wrong amounts, and mismatched details before they delay payment. Raised $18.4M from Menlo Ventures, First Round Capital, and Brick & Mortar Ventures.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on billing volume and integrations
tools.results: Customers bill 6x faster; 30% reduction in invoice aging; saves average of 7 days per week across billing workflow. Two-week onboarding typical.
tools.integrations: Viewpoint Vista (two-way real-time sync), Procore Pay, Textura, GCPay, Foundation Software
tools.spoken: Siteline is the billing and pay application platform built exclusively for commercial specialty subcontractors. It automates AIA pay app preparation, lien waiver collection, and vendor compliance tracking, and uses AI to validate waivers and flag errors before they hold up payment. Customers bill six times faster and reduce invoice aging by thirty percent on average, saving the equivalent of seven days per week across their billing workflow. Siteline integrates with Viewpoint Vista, Procore Pay, Textura, and GCPay. Pricing is custom-quoted based on billing volume.

tools.name: Document Crunch
tools.company: Document Crunch (pending acquisition by Trimble, announced April 2, 2026)
tools.description: AI contract review and risk reduction platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced acquisition on April 2, 2026, with close expected Q2 2026 and planned integration into Trimble Construction One.
tools.results: Reduces contract review time from hours to minutes; catches change order trigger language before execution. Acquisition by Trimble expected to deepen integration across the Trimble Construction One ecosystem once closed.
tools.integrations: Trimble Construction One (integration planned post-acquisition close); custom integrations with project management platforms
tools.spoken: Document Crunch is the AI contract review platform that reads construction contracts, specs, and insurance documents and surfaces risk clauses, change order triggers, and compliance obligations in plain language. Trimble announced its acquisition of Document Crunch on April 2, 2026, with the deal expected to close in the second quarter of 2026 and full integration planned into the Trimble Construction One ecosystem. Pricing is by custom quote and it serves both GC and specialty sub teams.

tools.name: Procore Change Orders with AI Assist
tools.description: AI-enhanced change order management within Procore platform that helps identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time alongside the project schedule and budget.
tools.results: Contractors using AI estimating report 22% fewer change orders from estimating errors; real-time financial impact tracking reduces disputes.
tools.integrations: Native to Procore; integrates with Autodesk, Sage, Viewpoint, and 400+ tools
tools.spoken: Procore's change order management with AI Assist lets project teams identify scope gaps, draft change order language, and track financial impacts in real time, all inside the same platform where the schedule and budget already live. Pricing is on annual construction volume.

tools.name: Pelles AI
tools.description: Generative AI platform for construction that creates RFIs, submittal logs, proposals, and other construction documents in the team's existing format, enabling smaller teams to produce at enterprise document volume.
tools.results: Handles more document volume with greater confidence; enables small teams to compete on documentation quality with larger firms.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations
tools.spoken: Pelles AI is a newer generative AI platform that can create RFIs, submittal logs, and proposals in your company's existing format, giving smaller teams the output speed of a much larger document production operation. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: BidIntell
tools.description: AI pre-bid scoring platform that anchors proposal decisions by evaluating bid fit before any estimating labor is invested, using BidIndex scoring on location, contract terms, GC history, and trade match.
tools.results: Average bid preparation time industry-wide down from 34 hours to 14 hours per project for AI-using contractors (Dodge Construction Network 2025).
tools.integrations: Plans for estimating and takeoff tool integrations
tools.spoken: BidIntell's pre-bid scoring also anchors the proposal phase, helping specialty subs invest estimating hours only on the invitations that fit their win profile before a single takeoff is started.

tools.name: Flowcase
tools.description: AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates, with AI assistance for translation, proofreading, and content tailoring.
tools.results: Strong for qualifications-based and design-assist competitions; reduces manual bid assembly time.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with project data systems
tools.spoken: Flowcase is an AI proposal management platform that matches company experience and employee qualifications to RFP requirements and auto-populates bid templates. It is especially strong for subs competing for design-assist and qualifications-based work. Pricing is custom-quoted.

tools.name: ContraVault
tools.description: AI pre-award workflow platform that takes teams from opportunity qualification through to submission-ready proposals with minimal manual effort, using AI to assemble, draft, and review proposal content.
tools.results: Contractors using AI estimating and proposal tools report 19% fewer cost overruns and 22% fewer change orders from estimating errors (Dodge 2025).
tools.integrations: Custom integrations
tools.spoken: ContraVault takes a team from opportunity qualification all the way to a submission-ready proposal using AI to assemble, draft, and review content with minimal manual effort. It is built for both GC and specialty sub teams competing for complex project awards. Pricing is by custom quote. A 2025 Dodge Construction Network survey found that average bid preparation time has dropped from thirty-four hours to fourteen hours per project for contractors using AI-powered estimating, and those contractors reported nineteen percent fewer cost overruns and twenty-two percent fewer change orders traced to estimating errors.

tools.name: ALICE Technologies
tools.description: AI construction planning and scheduling platform that simulates millions of possible build sequences to find the most time and cost-efficient path, with dynamic rescheduling when project conditions change. In April 2026, formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company to deploy generative scheduling across 35+ capital project clients in infrastructure, data centers, and energy, achieving schedule accelerations of up to 20%.
tools.results: $32M in revenue protected on a hyperscale data center project after a 30-day delay. McKinsey data center client reduced schedule by 40%. Documented: 17% reduction in project duration, 14% labor cost savings, 12% equipment cost savings. Deployed with 35+ clients via McKinsey partnership.
tools.integrations: Oracle Primavera P6, Oracle Primavera Cloud, Autodesk; McKinsey alliance for enterprise capital project delivery
tools.spoken: ALICE Technologies is the most thoroughly documented AI scheduling platform in commercial construction. It simulates millions of possible build sequences to surface the most time and cost efficient path, and adjusts dynamically when conditions on the job change. In April 2026, ALICE formalized a partnership with McKinsey and Company to bring generative scheduling to capital project clients across infrastructure, data centers, and energy, introducing the technology to more than thirty-five clients and achieving schedule accelerations of up to twenty percent. A McKinsey data center client reduced its entire construction schedule by forty percent. Earlier documented results include a seventeen percent reduction in project duration, fourteen percent labor cost savings, and twelve percent equipment cost savings. Pricing is by custom quote, and ALICE works alongside Primavera P6 and Oracle Primavera Cloud schedules.

tools.name: Phoenix Project Manager
tools.description: CPM scheduling software built for specialty contractors requiring resource-loaded critical path schedules without Primavera P6 complexity. Labor histogram view shows workforce demand across the full project portfolio.
tools.results: Purpose-built for electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors managing multi-project labor demand; significantly lower cost and learning curve than Primavera P6.
tools.integrations: Standard CPM schedule import/export formats
tools.spoken: Phoenix Project Manager is built for specialty contractors who need resource-loaded critical path schedules without the complexity of Primavera P6. Its labor histogram view lets electrical, mechanical, and plumbing contractors see workforce demand across their entire project portfolio. Starting at eighteen hundred dollars per year, it is one of the most accessible CPM scheduling tools purpose-built for specialty subs.

tools.name: Oracle Primavera P6
tools.description: Industry-standard CPM scheduling platform for large complex projects, with AI capabilities being extended through ALICE Technologies integration and Oracle's own Construction and Engineering Advisor suite.
tools.results: Widely required by GCs and owners on large commercial and infrastructure projects; AI extended through ALICE integration.
tools.integrations: ALICE Technologies, Oracle Construction Suite, Procore, Autodesk
tools.spoken: Oracle Primavera P6 remains the industry standard on large complex projects at roughly twenty-one hundred to twenty-five hundred dollars per user per year, and its AI capabilities are being extended through ALICE and third-party integrations.

tools.name: nPlan
tools.description: AI construction schedule forecasting and analytics platform trained on 750,000 historical construction schedules representing over $2 trillion of construction spend. Forecasts the uncertainty of every schedule activity, automatically identifies schedule integrity issues, and creates new schedules from scope documentation using generative AI.
tools.results: Forecasts schedule uncertainty at an activity level with accuracy unattainable by manual analysis; recognized as best-in-class for data-driven forecasting on large complex projects.
tools.integrations: Oracle Primavera P6, Primavera Cloud, Microsoft Project
tools.spoken: nPlan is an AI construction schedule forecasting platform trained on seven hundred fifty thousand historical construction schedules representing over two trillion dollars of construction spend. It forecasts the uncertainty of every activity in a schedule, automatically identifies schedule integrity issues, and creates new schedules from scope documentation using generative AI, giving project controls teams a level of accuracy unattainable by manual analysis. Pricing is by custom quote.

tools.name: SmartPM
tools.description: AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to automatically flag delays, variances, and critical path risks. Transforms raw schedule data into actionable dashboards and auto-generated reports without requiring a dedicated scheduling specialist.
tools.pricing: $400/project/month for advanced analytics; unlimited users per project; Essentials tier available
tools.results: Automates advanced project controls analytics; provides interactive web-based Gantt views and look-ahead/retrospective timeline comparisons; real-time delay and variance flagging.
tools.integrations: Autodesk Construction Cloud, Procore, Oracle Primavera P6
tools.spoken: SmartPM is an AI-driven automated project controls and schedule analytics platform that integrates with existing CPM scheduling software to flag delays, variances, and critical path risks in real time. It transforms raw scheduling data into automated reports and actionable dashboards without requiring a scheduling specialist. Pricing starts at four hundred dollars per project per month, and it integrates with Autodesk Construction Cloud and Procore.

tools.name: Outbuild
tools.description: AI-enabled collaborative scheduling and field coordination platform built for construction, connecting master schedules to lookahead planning for field teams. Trusted by 400+ customers managing $50B+ in active projects. Pricing scales by annual construction volume rather than per user or per project.
tools.pricing: Scales by Annual Construction Volume; example: ~$100/project/month or ~$50/user/month for 10 active projects with 20 users; 30-day free trial; Enterprise plan available
tools.results: Master schedules and lookahead plans stay connected with field updates syncing automatically; 400+ customers with $50B+ active projects on platform.
tools.integrations: Procore (directory, RFIs, submittals)
tools.spoken: Outbuild is an AI-enabled collaborative scheduling platform trusted by four hundred or more customers with over fifty billion dollars in active projects. Teams create master schedules and lookahead plans in a connected cloud environment where field updates sync back automatically while schedulers maintain control. Pricing scales based on annual construction volume rather than per user or per project, making it a practical option for growing specialty sub teams, with a thirty-day free trial available. It integrates with Procore.

tools.name: Intenseye
tools.description: AI workplace safety platform using existing camera infrastructure to detect 50+ categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time, including PPE violations, restricted zone entry, and ergonomic hazards, with automated compliance reporting. Has raised over $154M in total funding and operates in 25+ countries. New mobile app released; LLM integration and AI video anonymization in development.
tools.pricing: Custom quote based on camera count and facility size
tools.results: Companies using AI safety monitoring report incident reductions of 40-50%; 8 hours of footage reviewed in under 15 minutes. Deployed across 25+ countries. $154M+ in total funding.
tools.integrations: Works with existing camera infrastructure; custom integrations with safety management systems; new mobile app
tools.spoken: Intenseye is an AI video analytics platform that monitors existing camera feeds and detects more than fifty categories of unsafe acts and conditions in real time, including PPE violations, restricted zone entry, and ergonomic hazards. The company has now raised over one hundred fifty-four million dollars in total funding and operates across more than twenty-five countries. It has released a new mobile app and is adding large language model capabilities. Pricing is by custom quote based on camera count.

tools.name: Voxel AI
tools.description: AI-powered video analytics safety platform for workplace hazard detection and real-time safety event monitoring. Total funding now at $80M including a $44M Series B. Has expanded to seven new countries and multiple new international markets. Priced at 30-50% lower than comparable enterprise platforms, structured around camera count.
tools.pricing: Custom quote; estimated 30-50% less than Intenseye; structured by camera count
tools.results: Practical entry point for smaller contractors deploying on 1-3 active sites; provides video evidence documentation of safety events. $80M total funding; expanded to 7 new countries.
tools.integrations: Existing camera infrastructure; custom EHS platform integrations
tools.spoken: Voxel AI offers AI-powered safety monitoring with total funding now at eighty million dollars including a forty-four million dollar Series B, and has expanded operations to seven new countries. Pricing is structured around camera count at an estimated thirty to fifty percent lower cost than comparable enterprise platforms.

tools.name: Oracle Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety
tools.description: AI-enabled predictive safety intelligence solution launched in March 2026 that forecasts where safety incidents are likely to occur on construction projects before they happen, enabling proactive prevention for large GC and owner teams.
tools.pricing: Custom quote as part of Oracle Construction and Engineering suite
tools.results: Shifts safety from reactive to proactive model; predictive incident identification before events occur.
tools.integrations: Oracle Construction and Engineering platform; Oracle Primavera
tools.spoken: Oracle launched its Construction and Engineering Advisor for Safety in March 2026, adding predictive analytics that forecast where safety incidents are likely before they happen. It is aimed at large GC and owner teams running complex projects with exposure to high-frequency incident patterns.

tools.name: Pillar Technologies
tools.description: Connected device and sensor platform for construction site risk management, combining environmental sensors (temperature, moisture, atmosphere) with visual monitoring for real-time hazard alerts and site risk intelligence.
tools.results: Provides real-time multi-factor risk data; alerts teams to environmental conditions before they become safety incidents.
tools.integrations: Custom integrations with project management and safety platforms
tools.spoken: Pillar Technologies uses connected environmental sensors alongside visual monitoring to deliver real-time construction site risk management, with alerts covering temperature, moisture, atmospheric conditions, and visual hazard detection. Pricing is custom-quoted and it is primarily built for general contractors.

tools.name: DroneDeploy Safety AI
tools.description: AI safety monitoring module within DroneDeploy's reality capture platform that analyzes drone imagery and 360-degree site camera data against all OSHA standards 1910 and 1926 to detect hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations. Reports visible safety risks at 95% accuracy.
tools.pricing: $349/month (Individual plan, billed annually); $649/month (Advanced plan with full AI analytics, billed annually); enterprise custom pricing
tools.results: Beta customers reduced occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to 89% within 3 weeks; issues mapped to specific site locations and OSHA safety categories.
tools.integrations: DroneDeploy Reality Hub (drone maps, 360 ground imagery, interior walkthroughs); Procore; CompanyCam
tools.spoken: DroneDeploy Safety AI analyzes drone imagery and site camera data against OSHA standards to detect construction hazards including missing guardrails, exposed edges, and PPE violations, with reports accurate to ninety-five percent. Beta customers reduced the occurrence of unsafe conditions by up to eighty-nine percent within three weeks of deployment. Pricing for the individual plan starts at three hundred forty-nine dollars per month billed annually, with advanced AI analytics on the six-hundred-forty-nine-dollar plan.

tools.name: viAct
tools.description: AI video analytics safety platform that transforms existing site cameras into intelligent safety monitors using 200+ specialized detection modules. Detects PPE violations, fall risks, unsafe acts at height, housekeeping hazards, restricted zone breaches, and worker-equipment proximity risks in real time. Also functions as a digital EHS assistant automating incident reports and compliance documentation.
tools.results: 200+ AI detection modules covering comprehensive OSHA and site-specific safety categories; automates EHS documentation and safety trend analysis.
tools.integrations: Existing camera infrastructure; custom EHS and safety management system integrations
tools.spoken: viAct transforms existing site cameras into AI safety monitors using more than two hundred specialized detection modules covering PPE violations, fall risks, unsafe acts at height, restricted zone breaches, and worker-equipment proximity hazards. It also functions as a digital EHS assistant that automates incident reports, generates compliance documentation, and delivers real-time safety trend analysis. Pricing starts at two hundred dollars per camera per module per month, with a fourteen-day free trial.

tools.name: Fyld
tools.description: AI field operations platform that analyzes short video clips from jobsites to identify safety, quality, and delivery risks before they escalate. Raised $41M in a Series B round in February 2026. Reported 82% year-over-year growth in 2025. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, Emery Sapp & Sons, and Ferrovial. Primary focus on infrastructure and field-intensive specialty operations.
tools.results: 82% YoY growth in 2025. Contractors report up to 48% reduction in serious workplace incidents. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, and Emery Sapp & Sons. $41M Series B raised February 2026.
tools.integrations: Mobile-first platform; custom integrations with field operations and safety management systems
tools.spoken: Fyld is an AI field operations platform that analyzes short video clips from jobsites to identify safety, quality, and delivery risks before they escalate. It raised forty-one million dollars in a Series B round in February 2026 and reported eighty-two percent year-over-year growth in 2025. Customers include Kiewit, Quanta Services, and Emery Sapp and Sons. Contractors using the platform report reductions in serious workplace incidents of up to forty-eight percent. Pricing is by custom quote and it is built for specialty contractors and infrastructure operators managing field operations at scale.

market_context.owner_expectations: Major GCs are increasingly requiring specialty subcontractors to actively manage submittals, RFIs, and daily documentation inside Procore or Autodesk Build as a condition of project participation. BIM coordination and model-based submittals are expected on commercial projects above $10M. Digital daily reports with photo documentation and real-time cost tracking are moving from nice-to-have to contractual obligation. Subs who cannot interface digitally with GC platforms are being filtered from bid lists before the first conversation happens. AI adoption in construction has doubled year over year — 38% of contractors now report measurable business impact from AI, up from 17% just one year prior. Of those using AI, 94% plan to increase usage in 2026. Procore serves 17,500+ customers across hundreds of billions in construction volume, with GC-driven platform adoption increasing switching costs for subs.
market_context.adoption_cost: For a specialty sub with 5-20 project managers, a functional AI stack covering estimating, field documentation, submittal management, and billing automation typically runs $15,000-$80,000/year in software costs. A bundled platform approach (Procore) vs. best-of-breed point solutions is the primary cost variable. Training represents an additional 15-20% of total investment and is the most consistently underestimated cost. Implementation takes 60-90 days to reach operational efficiency. AI project management tools for small teams run $1,000-$3,000/year per category.
market_context.roi: 2025 Bluebeam industry report: 68% of AI early adopters saved at least $50,000; 46% recovered 500-1,000 hours of labor. Average industry ROI is 3.5x with top performers at 10x. Bid prep time down 60% (34 hours to 14 hours per project per Dodge 2025). Clark Construction documented $3.2M in project savings through AI risk management. AI scheduling reduces delays 20-30%. 19% fewer cost overruns and 22% fewer change orders for AI-estimating users. ENR reports contractors automating billing workflows recover 8-12 administrative hours per week. 38% of contractors now report measurable AI business impact, up from 17% one year prior.
